key_provisions: No formal university-wide AI policy as of April 2026
AI Task Force (chaired by Robert Cummings since 2022) charged with articulating guidance and best practices for the Provost
AI Policy Advisory Board under Academic Innovation (Marc Watkins) developing policy recommendations
Center for Excellence in Teaching and Learning provides syllabus policy templates for generative AI (faculty discretion model)
Library AI Committee provides faculty/student guidance on appropriate AI use
ai_policy.summary: Ole Miss has no single published university-wide AI policy; instead it operates a decentralized, faculty-discretion model. An AI Task Force (est. 2022) and an AI Policy Advisory Board are tasked with articulating best-practice guidance for the Provost, with recommendations targeted for spring 2025. CETL and the Libraries provide interim syllabus templates and usage guidance for faculty and students.

governance_structure.committee_name: University of Mississippi Artificial Intelligence Task Force
governance_structure.notes: Task Force has met quarterly since 2022, chaired by Robert Cummings, formed to coincide with the SEC Artificial Intelligence Consortium. Members include faculty, data scientists, and Office of Research and Sponsored Programs staff. Meeting recordings are archived on eGrove. A separate AI Policy Advisory Board led by Marc Watkins (Academic Innovation) provides policy recommendations. Membership rosters are not publicly posted.

vendor_deployments.product: NextGenAI research consortium (ChatGPT-family access for research)
vendor_deployments.notes: One of 15 institutions in OpenAI's NextGenAI consortium; one of only three SEC schools selected (alongside the charter group). Access tied to research through the National Center for Narrative Intelligence, Center for Practical Ethics, and Institute for Data Science. Not a campus-wide ChatGPT Edu deployment.

ai_academic_programs.name: B.S. Computer Science (AI coursework, no dedicated AI concentration confirmed)
M.S. in Computer and Information Science (AI emphasis available)

notable_deals_investments.title: NextGenAI Consortium membership
notable_deals_investments.summary: $50,000 research grant to UM as one of 15 founding NextGenAI institutions (part of OpenAI's $50M consortium). Funds competitive interdisciplinary AI research grants through the National Center for Narrative Intelligence, Center for Practical Ethics, and Institute for Data Science.

notable_deals_investments.title: State of Mississippi - NVIDIA AI partnership (statewide MOU)
notable_deals_investments.summary: Statewide MOU with NVIDIA covering IHL institutions (including UM) via the Mississippi AI Network (MAIN). Provides NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute resources, certifications, and training pathways. Not a direct UM-specific agreement.

notable_deals_investments.title: Mississippi AI Talent Accelerator Program (MAI-TAP) grants
notable_deals_investments.summary: $9.1M statewide grant program to IHL institutions to build AI workforce pipelines. UM is eligible as an IHL institution; specific UM allocation not confirmed in public sources.

leadership_statements.speaker: John Higginbotham
leadership_statements.role: Vice Chancellor for Research and Economic Development, University of Mississippi
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: This collaboration strengthens our commitment to innovation and positions our campus as a leader.

leadership_statements.speaker: Wes Jennings
leadership_statements.role: Co-Director, National Center for Narrative Intelligence, UM
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: AI can do things in minutes that would take researchers hundreds of hours; OpenAI reached out directly in November 2024.

state_ai_policy_context.summary: Governor Tate Reeves signed Executive Order No. 1584 on January 8, 2025 (Mississippi's first AI executive order), directing the Department of Information Technology Services to inventory state AI use and develop responsible-use policy recommendations. The order targets state agencies; it does not explicitly bind IHL universities but sets a climate for coordination. Separately, the 2024 Artificial Intelligence in Education Task Force Act focuses on K-12. The IHL Board has not published a system-wide AI policy. Statewide coordination flows through the Mississippi AI Network (MAIN), which includes IHL, and the state's NVIDIA MOU (2025). MS also enacted content-filter/student-data requirements for K-12 AI use.

narrative_summary: Ole Miss occupies a middle tier on AI posture among SEC peers: it has clear research-side momentum but no published enterprise AI policy and no chief AI officer. Its signature win is selection (March 2025) as one of 15 institutions in OpenAI's NextGenAI consortium and one of only three SEC schools in the charter group, bringing a modest $50K research grant routed through the National Center for Narrative Intelligence, Center for Practical Ethics, and Institute for Data Science. Governance runs through an AI Task Force (chaired by Robert Cummings since 2022) and an AI Policy Advisory Board under Academic Innovation (Marc Watkins), which were charged with delivering Provost-level best-practice guidance by spring 2025; no resulting university-wide policy has been publicly posted as of April 2026. In the classroom, Ole Miss uses a faculty-discretion model backed by CETL syllabus templates and a Libraries AI Committee. Enterprise tooling is notably quiet: no public evidence of a Microsoft 365 Copilot, ChatGPT Edu, or Google Gemini campus-wide rollout. New CIO Victoria Farnsworth (Aug 2025), who previously served as 'AI program lead' at UT Chattanooga, is positioned to accelerate enterprise AI. State context: Governor Reeves' January 2025 EO 1584 and the $9.1M MAI-TAP grants create pull for workforce-aligned AI programs statewide.
confidence_notes: HIGH confidence on: OpenAI NextGenAI membership and funding, AI Task Force existence/chair, CIO identity, state EO. MEDIUM confidence on: academic program scope (AI as coursework/emphasis, not a dedicated AI major/minor confirmed); exact task force membership not public. LOW/UNKNOWN: no public evidence of enterprise Copilot/ChatGPT Edu deployment, specific UM slice of MAI-TAP funding, whether the AI Policy Advisory Board delivered final recommendations to the Provost, enrollment figure is the commonly reported Fall 2024 total and may have shifted.
